reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification) PASSED: Phase 03's analysis is factually correct. Line 58 in handleColumnSidecarUnavailability has inverted logic - the comment on line 57 states 'There are zero blobs for that column index' but the code checks 'if (blobsCount > 0) return;', meaning it returns WITHOUT error when blobs EXIST but custody columns are missing. This violates the spec requirement from 01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (lines 1097-1112): 'All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included.' An attacker can trigger this via P2P data_column_sidecars_by_range requests by requesting custody columns for a block with blobs where the responding node is missing some custody columns. The node will yield partial sidecars instead of throwing RESOURCE_UNAVAILABLE, violating all-or-nothing consistency. TODO comments at lines 47-48 and 95-96 in dataColumnSidecarsByRange.ts confirm this error was intended to be thrown but is not implemented. Severity remains Medium as this affects data availability consistency for individual nodes (>5% network impact threshold).
reviewed_items.spec_reference: All sidecars of a block must be included in by_range response if any sidecar is included. (assertion: forall block, resp: any_sidecar_included(block, resp) => all_sidecars_included(block, resp))
